diagnostics.title: Missing effect dependencies
diagnostics.message: `useEffect` can run with a stale `setLedgerNodes, setLedgerEdges` & show your users old data.

diagnostics.title: Data fetching inside an effect
diagnostics.message: fetch() inside useEffect can race, double-fire, or leak. Use a data-fetching layer or Server Component instead.
diagnostics.help: Use a data-fetching layer or Server Component so fetches do not race, double-fire, or leak from `useEffect`.

diagnostics.title: Missing effect dependencies
diagnostics.message: `useEffect` can run with a stale `fetchRecords` & show your users old data.

diagnostics.title: await inside a loop
diagnostics.message: This makes the for…of loop slow because each await runs one after another, so collect the independent calls & run them together with `await Promise.all(items.map(...))`
diagnostics.help: Collect the items, then use `await Promise.all(items.map(...))` so independent work runs at the same time

diagnostics.title: Autofocus on an element
diagnostics.message: `autoFocus` moves focus on load, which can disrupt screen reader and keyboard users. Remove it and let users choose where to focus.
diagnostics.help: Do not use `autoFocus`. It disorients users on load.

diagnostics.title: Chained array iterations
diagnostics.message: This loops over your list twice because .filter().map() makes two passes, so do it in one pass with .reduce() or a for...of loop
diagnostics.help: Combine `.map().filter()` style chains into one pass with `.reduce()` or a `for...of` loop, so you only loop over the list once

diagnostics.title: Array index used as a key
diagnostics.message: Your users can see & submit the wrong data when this list reorders or filters, so use a stable id like `key={item.id}`, not the array index "index".
diagnostics.help: Use a stable id from the item, like `key={item.id}` or `key={item.slug}`. Index keys break when the list reorders or filters.

diagnostics.title: Many related useState calls
diagnostics.message: "PromptCalibrationDashboard" updates 5 separate useState values in one place — state that changes together is easier to keep consistent as a single useReducer action.
diagnostics.help: Group state that always changes together into `useReducer` so one dispatched action describes the whole transition instead of a list of setter calls.

diagnostics.title: .map().filter(Boolean) loops twice
diagnostics.message: This loops over your list twice because .map().filter(Boolean) makes two passes, so use .flatMap() to change & drop items in one pass
diagnostics.help: Use `.flatMap(item => condition ? [value] : [])` to change and drop items in one pass, instead of building a throwaway array in between

diagnostics.title: Array lookup inside a loop
diagnostics.message: This scales poorly because `array.includes()` inside a loop scans the whole list every time. Use a Set for constant-time lookups.
diagnostics.help: Use a `Set` or `Map` when you check for the same items over and over. `Array.includes`/`find` scans the whole list each time

diagnostics.title: Array lookup inside a loop
diagnostics.message: This scales poorly because `array.includes()` inside a loop scans the whole list every time. Use a Set for constant-time lookups.
diagnostics.help: Use a `Set` or `Map` when you check for the same items over and over. `Array.includes`/`find` scans the whole list each time

diagnostics.title: Chained array iterations
diagnostics.message: This loops over your list twice because .map().filter() makes two passes, so do it in one pass with .reduce() or a for...of loop
diagnostics.help: Combine `.map().filter()` style chains into one pass with `.reduce()` or a `for...of` loop, so you only loop over the list once

diagnostics.title: Intl formatter rebuilt each call
diagnostics.message: This is slow because new Intl.DateTimeFormat() rebuilds on every call inside a function, so move it to the top of the file, or wrap it in useMemo
diagnostics.help: Move `new Intl.NumberFormat(...)` to the top of the file or wrap it in `useMemo`. Building one is slow, so don't redo it on every call

diagnostics.title: Array index used as a key
diagnostics.message: Your users can see & submit the wrong data when this list reorders or filters, so use a stable id like `key={item.id}`, not the array index "index".
diagnostics.help: Use a stable id from the item, like `key={item.id}` or `key={item.slug}`. Index keys break when the list reorders or filters.

diagnostics.title: Missing effect dependencies
diagnostics.message: `useEffect` can run with a stale `labels` & show your users old data.

diagnostics.title: Event logic handled in an effect
diagnostics.message: Faking an event handler with state plus a useEffect costs an extra render & runs late.
diagnostics.help: Run the side effect in the event handler that triggers it, instead of watching its state from a useEffect. See https://react.dev/learn/you-might-not-need-an-effect#sharing-logic-between-event-handlers

diagnostics.title: Pure function rebuilt every render
diagnostics.message: `copyText` inside `useTaskFlowController` uses no local state but is rebuilt on every render, so it wastes work & breaks memoized children. Move it to the top of the file, outside the component.
diagnostics.help: Move the function above the component, at the top of the file. It doesn't use local state, so rebuilding it each update is wasted work.

diagnostics.title: Static value rebuilt every render
diagnostics.message: `filterOptions` inside `TaskFlowControls` uses no local state but is rebuilt every render, so it looks new each time & breaks memoized children. Move it to the top of the file, outside the component.
diagnostics.help: Move the value above the component, at the top of the file. It doesn't use local state, so rebuilding it each update is wasted and makes it look new every time.

diagnostics.title: Non-component export in component file
diagnostics.message: This file exports non-components, so Fast Refresh can't safely preserve component state.
diagnostics.help: Move non-component exports out of component files so Fast Refresh can preserve component state instead of full-reloading.

diagnostics.title: Intl formatter rebuilt each call
diagnostics.message: This is slow because new Intl.DateTimeFormat() rebuilds on every call inside a function, so move it to the top of the file, or wrap it in useMemo
diagnostics.help: Move `new Intl.NumberFormat(...)` to the top of the file or wrap it in `useMemo`. Building one is slow, so don't redo it on every call

diagnostics.title: Static value rebuilt every render
diagnostics.message: `subscribedChannels` inside `useTopologyController` uses no local state but is rebuilt every render, so it looks new each time & breaks memoized children. Move it to the top of the file, outside the component.
diagnostics.help: Move the value above the component, at the top of the file. It doesn't use local state, so rebuilding it each update is wasted and makes it look new every time.

diagnostics.title: Pure function rebuilt every render
diagnostics.message: `generateSparklinePath` inside `useTopologyController` uses no local state but is rebuilt on every render, so it wastes work & breaks memoized children. Move it to the top of the file, outside the component.
diagnostics.help: Move the function above the component, at the top of the file. It doesn't use local state, so rebuilding it each update is wasted work.

diagnostics.title: Array lookup inside a loop
diagnostics.message: This scales poorly because `array.includes()` inside a loop scans the whole list every time. Use a Set for constant-time lookups.
diagnostics.help: Use a `Set` or `Map` when you check for the same items over and over. `Array.includes`/`find` scans the whole list each time
